name: Pasta Flora
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour into a bowl and mix in baking powder.
2. Add butter, egg yolk, sugar, cognac, and lemon peel; knead to form a soft dough.
3. Divide the dough into two parts.
4. Roll one part out into a thick crust and place it in a square pan.
5. Break off a piece from the remaining half and roll between your palms into a coil to fit around the crust.
6. Wet the edge of the crust with water and gently press the coil around the edges, adhering it to the crust and sides of the pan; shape with fingers to form the side crust.
7. Spread marmalade over the bottom crust.
8. Shape additional coils, each about as thick as your little finger, from the rest of the dough and lay them on the marmalade in a crisscross latticework pattern.
9. Join the edges with a little water.
10. Bake in a preheated oven at 300°F for 30 to 40 minutes.
11. Cool and cut into squares.
